mechanism_narrative: A decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future equity cash flows, directly increasing present valuations of S&P 500 constituents over the next month.
failure_condition: Rank IC for FEDFUNDS is negative or insignificant (p > 0.10) over any 2-year rolling window, or forward returns do not increase when FEDFUNDS declines.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts falling below 1.6 million units signals a severe contraction in residential investment and consumer confidence, predicting negative equity returns over the next quarter due to expected earnings downgrades.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 60-day returns in > 65% of instances where HOUST is below 1600, or the signal loses predictive power (Rank IC < 0.02) in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: The simultaneous rise in the unemployment rate above 3.5% and a decline in total non-farm payrolls indicates a broad-based labor market deterioration, triggering negative risk sentiment and lower forward returns.
failure_condition: The joint condition (UNRATE > 3.5 AND PAYEMS < 152500) fails to predict negative returns with statistical significance (t-stat < 1.5) over a 5-year walk-forward window.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in the index of industrial production reflects immediate weakness in corporate output and capacity utilization, leading to short-term negative revisions in earnings expectations.
failure_condition: The sign of the 5-day forward return is positive in > 55% of periods where INDPRO declines, or the autocorrelation of the signal is too high to provide incremental alpha.

mechanism_narrative: High consumer price inflation (CPIAUCSL > 290) combined with low policy rates (FEDFUNDS < 2.0) creates a stagflationary regime where real rates are deeply negative, compressing equity valuations due to uncertainty and margin pressure.
failure_condition: Equity returns are not significantly lower (p > 0.05) in the stagflationary regime compared to neutral regimes, or the interaction term is insignificant in a multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: Real state and local government debt (RSAFS) falling below 680,000 million dollars indicates a credit crunch and reduced fiscal stimulus, signaling weak aggregate demand and negative equity returns.
failure_condition: The threshold signal (RSAFS < 680000) fails to predict negative returns with a hit rate > 50% over a 3-year rolling window, or the series exhibits structural breaks that invalidate the threshold.

mechanism_narrative: An elevated unemployment rate (UNRATE > 4.0%) serves as a lagging but persistent indicator of economic weakness, predicting lower corporate earnings and negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The correlation between UNRATE and 60-day forward returns is positive or insignificant (p > 0.10) over any 5-year period, refuting the negative earnings channel.

mechanism_narrative: The combination of GDP growth (GDPC1) falling below 1.0% and housing starts (HOUST) below 1.6 million units confirms a deep demand collapse, predicting severe negative equity returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The joint condition fails to predict negative returns with a hit rate > 60% in out-of-sample testing, or the marginal contribution of the interaction term is insignificant.
